One of the themes that we've been talking about a lot is variety. Don't just do the same thing over again. If you do a lot of problem solution, do star story solution. If you do a lot of star story solution, do problem solution. So don't just write long headlines. It seems like a lot of people write the same length headline every time. Don't forget you've got a headline, right? Big headline like that. Then you can have a subhead that kind of explains things a little more. And then you can even have a subhead that explains things a little more. So you've got, think of this as like valuable real estate that you can use, right? In different ways. So sometimes the best headline is like maybe just two words. Someone in United States, Arthur Johnson once had a headline. I think I mentioned yesterday, had enough question mark? Had enough of people telling you what you can and can't do, what you can eat, and you can't eat. We talked about a headline for, I think for Stani's letter. I can, right? All the things you can now do. So I can, each can. And then in the subhead you could explain blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. Now you can do all the things you couldn't do before. And your money, your health, all these wonderful things. But just that big words like that. Eat, const or whatever it is. It stops someone on the page. It's like, wow, it just goes right, goes like right into their brain. Whereas if you have a headline like, you know, how to have a wonderful life by combining wonderful things about, about health and financial all in one newsletter. It's like, oh, I have to read that. That looks like a lot of work. But you don't even have to decide to read, I can, right? You just look at it, you go, oh, I can, goes right into your brain. So when you think about headlines, when you do your hundred, like I hope you do every time you have to write a headline, do the hundred headlines and make some of them little short little headlines. Two words, one word. What's one word? You know, wrong. Why everything you believe about investing, about alternative health, about being an HR manager. Everything you believe about that is wrong. And, you know, the real truth revealed by an expert. Now that you've had the experience of actually writing some, I wanted to show you just a couple of formulas that it boils down to. And maybe that'll help you think of a few more as you're sitting or as we're going around. But different bullets take on the form of basically how to, right? A simplest kind of a bullet. How to do this. How to do this incredible thing that you want to do. And then sometimes it's how to do this, how to do whatever with blank, with this additional thing, right? How to cure arthritis with the fastest, easiest way possible, right? How to cure arthritis with a paper clip. How to cure arthritis with a spice that you already have in your home. Right? What's just tell a little bit more about how to do it. How to do X, even if, how to cure arthritis, even if you've had it for years. How to cure arthritis, even if your doctor says you're beyond hope. Even if you've tried every cure out there. How to cure arthritis without, right? How to cure arthritis without drugs. How to cure arthritis without surgery. How to cure anything that you can tell someone to do. How to do it without the things that they don't want to have along with it, like hard work, for instance. Or expense. How to become a better purchasing manager, you know, without ever paying another penny for expensive courses and books. The best way, the only way to, the right way to, the wrong way to. What can we say that will make it so that it's interesting to them because it's the best way, it's the only way. I want to know the best way, I want to know the only way. I want to know the wrong way. Maybe I'm doing the wrong thing. And then the first thing we talked about, what never to do, what always to do. What never to do in an, what never to eat in an airplane. What never to do when you're firing someone. What never to do when you're investing in options. What always to do with, when you're investing in options. And then this is a great little formula. The best way to invest in stocks is like this, right? Wrong. That's exactly the wrong thing to do. You do that and it's trouble. So what gets people's attention. What sets the, what sets the page on fire, like we talked about. What sets the page on fire is something that I think is true that's not true. Like, whoa, I got to know that. I don't want to make a fool of myself. I don't want to be wrong about that. I don't want to be mistaken. That can cost me money. That can cost me embarrassment. That could cost me my health. Want to blank? Want to cure your arthritis? Why you must stop taking drugs immediately? Want to get rich in the stock market? Why you must stop listening to those people on TV? Or why you must do the opposite of what, it's always good when it's like the opposite of what they think, right? In English we say counterintuitive. If I want to invest in stocks, I should invest in stocks that are going to go up. And maybe I should not do that. If you want to get rich, here's why you should never invest in a stock that you think is going to go up. And maybe there's some reason for doing that. Want to cure your arthritis? Here's why you should never take, like, glucosamine or some common arthritis remedy that everyone thinks they should take. Well, you shouldn't take it because there's something better. And we're going to tell you about what that is. Got blank, right? You know, got diabetes? Do you have arthritis? Want to get rich in the stock market? Want to do better at what you're doing for your job? Here's how to do it. Here's how to get rid of it, right? Got arthritis? Here are five ways to make your arthritis vanish overnight. The biggest mistake, right? Everyone wants to know what's the biggest mistake people make? The biggest blunder, right? The biggest mistake people make about investing? What's the biggest mistake people make about options? In health it's always good because, you know, big mistake can lead to you, literally dying. You know, the big mistake people make when they go in for surgery. If you do this, your odds of dying on the operating table increase by two times. I want to know what that is. I want to know what that is in case I get an operation. Want to know what that is in case my mother gets an operation. Anyone I love gets an operation. I want to tell my friends about it. Why you should blank, right? And it's a surprise. Why you should, you know, stop answering your phone. Why you should stop investing in the stock market. Why you should stop taking herbs for your health. Why you should stop taking vitamins. What? I should stop taking vitamins? You know, well, yes, you should stop taking vitamins because maybe they don't do anything. Maybe you have a better way. Maybe the vitamins don't get absorbed and you have a way to help vitamins get absorbed, right? Always try to tell people something that's really unexpected. And then fantasy. One of the best ways to come up with a headline or to come up with a bullet is to say, what if I could come up with the one thing that I could promise them? Whether it's true or not, what would it be, right? And so for a tax thing, the idea of how to pay zero taxes. That's like what I, if I had a tax newsletter, that's what I'd like my headline to be. How to pay zero taxes. Now I got to think about how do I make that be true? Well, there probably are some ways in certain circumstances to pay zero taxes. You have enough deductions. You make little enough income. You take your income and you put it in a business. There are some ways of doing that. But then I would say, yes, there are some ways where we could get your tax down to absolutely zero. But it's a lot of work and maybe you don't have the circumstances to do that. But wouldn't it be great if we got your taxes down by 50% if we cut your taxes in half? Wouldn't it be great if we only, you know, if we didn't get it to zero, but we only got it to like 10% or something? So you can kind of make an interesting topic that gets them into it, that attracts them, fulfill it a little bit, but then talk about, well, not everyone can pay zero taxes. So here's what you can do, right? How never to be sick, right? Like if I saw that headline, I would want to read about how never to be sick. And so then maybe it says, well, here, you know, if you never want to be sick, do this, do this. It builds your immune system, blah, blah, blah. Of course, you know, sometimes you may get sick. You know, I can't say you'll never get sick, absolutely. But if you do get sick, we also have ways of making you better, you know, really overnight, really quickly. So for each of your products, right, think about if you could promise anything, if you could promise the prospect anything at all, what would it be? And then how close can I come to that, right? How close can I come to telling a purchasing manager you'll never make a mistake on buying raw materials in advance again, trying to figure out what the price will be. How close can I come to telling an investor you'll never lose money? You'll always make money. How close can I come to telling a person with arthritis you'll completely get rid of your arthritis? And when you do that, it gives you the basis for headlines that can be very powerful and bullets that can be very powerful.
